title: Towards a Decolonised Science in South Africa
outline: This course addresses key topics relevant to the decolonization of science in South Africa. Course objectives include: (1) contrasting the science behind human diversity (e.g. skin colour variation, sex) with socially-constructed categories (e.g. race, gender binary); (2) demonstrating how the history of racism and gender bias has limited the focus of scientific enquiry; (3) highlighting the importance of diversity and diverse voices in the production of scientific knowledge; (4) showing how African voices have shaped and are continuing to shape the trajectory of science. The approach is a blended learning environment combining online lectures, tutorials, tasks, and assessments. Guests from within UCT and from further afield supplement material through lectures, interviews and/or short case studies.
assessment: Class participation (workshops/tutorials and online discussions) 20%; online tests 20%; final exam 30%; final project 30%. Note: The 30% final exam allocation reflects the format and intensive interactive nature of the course; the final project (equal weight to the exam) will be externally examined.
